# Beudox HR Portal - Technical Context

## Architecture Overview

Beudox HR is a single-page application (SPA) built with React and TypeScript, following a component-based architecture. The application uses Supabase as a backend-as-a-service platform providing database, authentication, and serverless functions.

### Key Architectural Decisions

- **Frontend-First Approach**: All business logic resides in the React application with Supabase handling data persistence and authentication
- **Component Composition**: Extensive use of reusable UI components from shadcn/ui library with custom business components
- **Route-Based Navigation**: React Router DOM manages client-side routing with page components in `/src/pages/`
- **Real-time Data**: Leverages Supabase's real-time subscriptions for live updates (not extensively used in current codebase)
- **Mobile-Responsive Design**: Tailwind CSS ensures responsive layouts across devices

## Core Components and Patterns

### Layout System
- `AppLayout.tsx`: Main application layout with sidebar navigation and top bar
- `AppSidebar.tsx`: Collapsible sidebar with role-based menu items
- `TopBar.tsx`: Header with notifications, user menu, and search
- `MobileSidebarContext.tsx`: Context for managing mobile sidebar state

### Authentication Flow
- Supabase Auth handles user authentication
- `MandatoryPasswordChange.tsx`: Forces password reset for temporary accounts
- Role-based access control implemented through `role-access.ts` utilities
- Employee data fetched and cached using custom hooks

### Data Management
- Direct Supabase client usage throughout components
- Custom hooks for complex data fetching (not extensively shown in provided files)
- Real-time subscriptions for live data updates
- Optimistic updates for better UX

### UI Component Patterns
- Extensive use of shadcn/ui components (Button, Card, Dialog, etc.)
- Custom components like `SearchableEmployeeSelect` for complex interactions
- Consistent styling with CSS custom properties for theming
- Accessibility considerations with proper ARIA labels

## Key Files and Their Purposes

### Entry Points
- `src/main.tsx`: Application bootstrap, renders App component
- `src/pages/Index.tsx`: Root page component (likely dashboard)
- `src/App.tsx`: Main App component with routing setup

### Core Utilities
- `src/lib/utils.ts`: Common utility functions (cn for className merging)
- `src/lib/role-access.ts`: Functions for checking user permissions
- `src/lib/format-date.ts`: Date formatting utilities
- `src/lib/attendance-format.ts`: Attendance-specific formatting
- `src/lib/client-activity.ts`: Activity logging and categorization

### Business Logic Components
- `AttendanceSummary.tsx`: Complex component displaying attendance metrics, overtime analysis, and anomaly detection
- Employee profile components: Modular sections for different HR aspects
- Settings components: Administrative configuration interfaces

## Data Flow and Business Logic

### Attendance Management
- Records stored in `attendance_records` table with fields for check-in/out times, working hours, overtime
- Automatic calculation of working days, attendance rates, and anomalies
- Support for regular and holiday overtime tracking
- Late arrival detection and reporting

### Employee Lifecycle
- Employee creation through invitation system (handled by Supabase Edge Functions)
- Mandatory password change on first login
- Profile management with tabs for different HR functions
- Status tracking (active/inactive) with deactivation workflows

### Leave Management
- Balance tracking across multiple leave types
- Request/approval workflow
- Automatic balance updates and validation

### Payroll Processing
- Integration with attendance data for automatic calculations
- Support for allowances, overtime pay, and deductions
- PDF generation for payslips (handled by Edge Functions)

## Database Schema Insights

Based on component usage, the database includes:

- `employees`: Core employee data with auth integration
- `attendance_records`: Time tracking data
- `leave_requests` & `leave_balances`: Leave management
- `payroll_records`: Salary and payment data
- `projects` & `project_assignments`: Project management
- `evaluations` & `evaluation_schedules`: Performance reviews
- `company_settings`: Organizational configuration
- `departments`, `roles`, `leave_types`: Reference data

## Security Considerations

- Row Level Security (RLS) policies in Supabase (not shown in frontend code)
- Role-based access control implemented in frontend
- Secure authentication flow with mandatory password changes
- API key management through environment variables

## Performance Patterns

- Lazy loading of components and data
- Efficient querying with proper indexes assumed in Supabase
- Memoization of expensive calculations (seen in AttendanceSummary)
- Optimistic UI updates for better perceived performance

## Development Workflow

- Vite for fast development and building
- TypeScript for type safety
- ESLint for code quality
- Playwright for E2E testing
- Git hooks for automated processes (implied by Tessera integration)

This architecture supports a scalable HR management system with clear separation of concerns and maintainable code patterns.
